data.body:
Correction (re-review round). The re-run flagged that my "REST-submittable vs FFO-landable" disambiguation had the mechanics INVERTED, contradicting ADR-0040. Verified against ADR-0040 (§ "submittable only if a descendant of the current main tip … the change becomes non-submittable until rebased"): under FFO, Gerrit's `submittable` ALREADY requires descendant-of-tip, so a behind-tip change is **non-submittable** (`submittable=false`), NOT `submittable=True`. Reframed S2 + S4 accordingly:
- The real distinctions are (1) a TOCTOU race — `submittable=True` is only true as of the read, so re-confirm just before `POST /submit` (FFO refuses a stale submit with "not fast-forward"); and (2) a native `submittable=false` doesn't say WHY (behind-tip → `needs_rebase` vs a failing vote → `ci_failed`/`review_failed`), which is why `needs_rebase` needs the bot's S3 marker.
- Dropped the misleading "REST-submittable / FFO-landable" terminology.

NOTE for reviewer: the re-review at the new head SHA surfaced that S2/S4/S5 still BLOCK on a substantial set of PRE-EXISTING design gaps well beyond this residual round (e.g. `/rebase:chain` is not a real Gerrit REST path; the `N=5` "park" mechanism is undefined; multi-member chain needs per-member Verified SHAs not a single `tested_sha`; CloudWatch has no native histogram-with-buckets; `autoheal` vs `restart: unless-stopped` for unhealthy; the autolander state volume + compose-up.sh SITE_SUBDIRS drift gate). The epic itself PASSES and is signed; the children do not. These are real design decisions, not wording — flagged for a scope decision rather than silently resolved here.
